other_observers: None; Yes, the original and only observer. Much time has passed, as I initally misidentified it in 2006 as a juvenile American White Pelican.
specific_location: 39.504306,-105.079594, The 2nd and larger of two ponds (old water filled gravel pits), just south of Chatfield Reservoir on the east side of the South Platte River. A location I am very familar with and have visited a few hundred times.
description: Pelicans are comparatively unmistakable in size shape and flight pattern. Large mostly gray bird with paler almost white underparts. Typical long, flat, pointed pelican type bill, mostly gray tinged with lighter brown.Behaviors: Lone individual, flying low, fairly fast, realtively flat trajectory. Made a couple of passes. On one pass it was it was followed closely by a Bald Eagle maybe 75-100 yards behind, same flight path and speed but did not attack. A pair of eagles were observed in the area earlier that day.  Finally it settled on the water for a brief period, then moved to the shore for preening. No feeding behaviour was observed.Call: SilentPlumage: Juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Lee Farrell, Lee Farrell, Lee Farrell, Lee Farrell, Lee Farrell
